NVDA.title: Qualcomm is buying Modular for $4 billion to take on Nvidia where it actually counts
NVDA.summary: Qualcomm is reportedly in advanced talks to acquire AI inference and compiler startup Modular Inc. for $4 billion. This acquisition aims to bolster Qualcomm's data center chip ambitions by providing a crucial software stack, including the MAX inference framework and Mojo programming language, to challenge Nvidia's dominance in AI infrastructure. The deal would be announced ahead of Qualcomm's Investor Day, demonstrating its commitment to expanding beyond mobile chips into the data center market by addressing the software challenges faced by developers.

NVDA.title: Coherent (NYSE:COHR) up 9% as AI optics squeeze takes focus, not grant size
NVDA.summary: Coherent (NYSE:COHR) stock rose 9.2% after securing a CHIPS Act letter of intent for up to $50 million. This funding will support the expansion of its 6-inch indium phosphide plant in Texas, which is critical for AI data centers and addresses supply chain concerns due to China's indium export controls. The rally highlights investor focus on increased indium phosphide capacity rather than just the grant amount, especially given Coherent's existing multiyear deal with Nvidia.

NVDA.title: Knowles Corp stock hits all-time high at 42.63 USD
NVDA.summary: Knowles Corporation (KN) stock reached an all-time high of $42.61, marking a 140% increase over the past year. The company boasts a market capitalization of $3.64 billion and a Piotroski Score of 9, signaling strong financial health. Despite appearing overvalued by InvestingPro analysis, the stock's year-to-date gains exceeding 93% and recent strong Q1 2026 financial results, including higher-than-forecasted EPS and revenue, reflect significant investor confidence.

NVDA.title: Should You Buy, Hold, or Sell Micron Stock Ahead of Q3 Earnings?
NVDA.summary: Micron Technology (MU) is set to release its fiscal third-quarter 2026 results, with strong guidance driven by high demand for AI-related memory, particularly high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips. The company projects significant revenue and EPS growth, while maintaining strong gross margins despite increased production. Analysts highlight Micron's attractive valuation compared to peers and its Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) status as reasons for investors to consider the stock.

NVDA.title: Lam Research Corp Stock (LRCX) Closed Up by 5.14% on Jun 22: A Full Analysis
NVDA.summary: Lam Research Corp (LRCX) saw a significant stock price increase of 5.14%, driven by a positive revision in industry growth forecasts and bullish analyst commentary, specifically from Wells Fargo. This surge is attributed to expanding AI infrastructure and advanced packaging technologies, increasing demand for wafer fabrication equipment. Despite strong financial performance and an optimistic outlook, the stock experiences intraday volatility due to valuation concerns and geopolitical risks, including its exposure in China.

NVDA.title: KLA Corp Stock (KLAC) Closed Up by 3.52% on Jun 22: A Full Analysis
NVDA.summary: KLA Corp (KLAC) stock surged by 3.52% on June 22, driven by increased liquidity and accessibility following a ten-for-one stock split earlier in the month. The company's dominance in semiconductor process control and inspection, fueled by rising AI and advanced packaging demands, along with positive analyst sentiment, contributed to the upward movement. Despite strong performance, the company faces risks such as extreme valuation multiples, insider selling, input cost pressures, and geopolitical export controls to China.

NVDA.title: Applied Materials Inc Stock (AMAT) Closed Up by 3.70% on Jun 22: Key Drivers Unveiled
NVDA.summary: Applied Materials Inc. (AMAT) stock closed up 3.70% on June 22, driven by record fiscal second-quarter revenue, high gross margins, and a strategic partnership with EssilorLuxottica for advanced augmented reality smart eyewear. Analysts have raised price targets due to strong AI demand and projected equipment growth. However, the stock experienced intraday volatility attributed to valuation concerns, profit-taking, and geopolitical risks.

NVDA.title: Lumentum Holdings (NASDAQ:LITE) rises as AI optics push lifts stock despite Nasdaq slump
NVDA.summary: Lumentum Holdings (NASDAQ:LITE) saw a significant stock rise of 6.1% despite a Nasdaq slump, driven by its strong position in AI optics with over $400 million in backlog and "underappreciated" co-packaged optics attach rates. The company's focus on optical circuit switches and CPO lasers for AI data centers is seen as a key growth driver, amplified by its inclusion in major indexes and a $2 billion investment from Nvidia. Despite high valuations, investors are increasingly viewing Lumentum as a crucial part of the AI infrastructure supply chain.

MSFT.title: Gartner: Half of Gen AI Projects Could Exceed Budget by 2028
MSFT.summary: Gartner predicts that at least 50% of generative AI projects will exceed their planned budgets by 2028 due to poor architectural choices and a lack of operational expertise. The primary cost driver is inference, which is expected to account for 70% of a model's lifetime costs, shifting focus from training expenses. The report emphasizes the need for cost governance, architectural efficiency, and usage monitoring to scale GenAI without unsustainable spending, especially as agentic AI further increases operational costs.

MSFT.title: Health Catalyst, Zoom, and Autodesk Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
MSFT.summary: Shares of Health Catalyst (HCAT), Zoom (ZM), and Autodesk (ADSK) experienced declines due to a general downturn in the communication services and software sector. This decline was triggered by AI talent departures from Alphabet and broader market fears that AI agents could erode the traditional subscription model of enterprise software. Despite mixed signals from company performance and analyst upgrades, the market remains cautious about the software sector until AI revenue growth demonstrably outpaces potential cannibalization of legacy services.

MSFT.title: Intuit, Akamai, and RingCentral Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
MSFT.summary: Shares of Intuit (INTU), Akamai (AKAM), and RingCentral (RNG) plummeted following a confluence of factors, including AI talent departures from Alphabet, regulatory concerns, and market fears that AI agents will erode traditional subscription-based software economics. The market's reaction, exacerbated by Accenture's recent growth outlook cut, highlights the ongoing debate about AI's impact on software vendors, even as companies like Salesforce show strong fundamentals and AI-driven growth. Geopolitical tensions, such as the Apache helicopter incident and perceived Iranian aggression, also added macro headwinds, disproportionately affecting long-duration assets like software stocks due to their sensitivity to interest rates.

MSFT.title: Oracle (ORCL) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
MSFT.summary: Oracle (ORCL) shares fell 5% due to a broader market decline in communication-services and software stocks, triggered by AI talent departures from Alphabet and regulatory concerns. The market's fear that AI agents will undermine the traditional subscription model of enterprise software has grown, leading to significant declines in other software companies like Salesforce and Adobe. This trend is exacerbated by confirmations from firms like Accenture that AI is compressing demand for traditional IT services, making investors wary of software vendors, despite some counterarguments about their intrinsic value.

MSFT.title: Manhattan business owner sues former employer Microsoft for $10M
MSFT.summary: David Gemoll, owner of Culver’s in Manhattan, has filed a $10 million wrongful termination lawsuit against Microsoft Corporation in the U.S. District Court of Kansas. Gemoll, who was hired by Microsoft in 2014 and promoted to a senior role in April 2020, claims he was wrongly terminated. His role involved managing significant commercial partnerships and automotive industry contracts for Microsoft's Azure Cloud and Artificial Intelligence organization.

MSFT.title: How 3 Top Dividend Managers Are Navigating Today’s Market
MSFT.summary: Three top dividend fund managers, Michael Barclay, Andrew Brandon, and Ramona Persaud, discussed the evolving landscape of dividend investing at the Morningstar Investment Conference. They explored the importance of quality over just yield, the relaxation of traditional dividend requirements to include growth companies, and the differing views on stock buybacks versus dividends as commitments to shareholders. The managers also identified currently interesting sectors like healthcare, while noting challenges in traditional dividend strongholds due to changing consumer loyalty and market dynamics.

MSFT.title: Roundhill MSFT WeeklyPay ETF announces weekly distribution of $0.0984
MSFT.summary: The Roundhill MSFT WeeklyPay ETF has announced a weekly distribution of $0.0984 per share. This distribution is set to be paid out to shareholders.

MSFT.title: Oracle Corp Stock (ORCL) Moved Down by 3.90% on Jun 22: What Signal Does It Send?
MSFT.summary: Oracle Corporation's stock (ORCL) fell by 3.90% primarily due to concerns over its negative free cash flow, massive infrastructure capital expenditure, and planned debt and equity financing for fiscal year 2027 which could lead to shareholder dilution. Despite strong recent earnings, investors are worried about the company's financial dynamics and conservative revenue guidance. Broader macroeconomic headwinds affecting tech stocks also contributed to the decline.

GOOGL.title: Form DEF 14A Alphabet Inc Class A For: 22 June By Investing.com
GOOGL.summary: This article announces the filing of Form DEF 14A by Alphabet Inc. Class A (GOOGL) for June 22. It is a brief, factual notification regarding a regulatory filing related to the company. The content primarily consists of the headline and a repetition of the announcement.

GOOGL.title: Wells Fargo Lifts DTE Energy (DTE) Price Target by $5. Here is Why
GOOGL.summary: Wells Fargo has increased its price target for DTE Energy (NYSE: DTE) from $160 to $165, maintaining an 'Overweight' rating, following a recent roadshow with DTE's CEO. This move signifies an almost 12% upside potential from the current stock price. The adjustment comes as DTE actively seeks new large-load deals, including plans for an additional 2 GW of data center capacity, building on existing partnerships with Oracle and Google.

GOOGL.title: The Real Price Of Admission For Broadcom Stock
GOOGL.summary: Broadcom stock (AVGO) appears expensive at first glance due to its high price-to-earnings multiple, but this perspective changes significantly when considering projected earnings growth over three years. Analysts forecast substantial growth, particularly in AI semiconductor revenue, which effectively lowers the true multiple a patient investor pays for future earnings. The article emphasizes that while the stock isn't "cheap," the current price doesn't overpay for its underlying growth, hinting at potential appreciation if the market continues to value it as a premier growth company.

GOOGL.title: GOOGL Stock Plummets As A24 Alliance Juxtaposes AI Brain Drain
GOOGL.summary: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) shares dropped more than 5% following the departure of two key AI researchers to rival companies, overshadowing its newly announced cinematic research partnership with film studio A24. This talent drain poses a significant challenge to Google DeepMind despite its strategic alliance with A24 to integrate AI into filmmaking. The collaboration aims to develop artist-guided filmmaking software and represents a growing intersection between Silicon Valley and Hollywood, but the stock decline highlights investor concerns over losing critical AI talent.

GOOGL.title: Health Catalyst, Zoom, and Autodesk Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
GOOGL.summary: Shares of Health Catalyst (HCAT), Zoom (ZM), and Autodesk (ADSK) experienced declines due to a general downturn in the communication services and software sector. This decline was triggered by AI talent departures from Alphabet and broader market fears that AI agents could erode the traditional subscription model of enterprise software. Despite mixed signals from company performance and analyst upgrades, the market remains cautious about the software sector until AI revenue growth demonstrably outpaces potential cannibalization of legacy services.

GOOGL.title: Oracle (ORCL) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
GOOGL.summary: Oracle (ORCL) shares fell 5% due to a broader market decline in communication-services and software stocks, triggered by AI talent departures from Alphabet and regulatory concerns. The market's fear that AI agents will undermine the traditional subscription model of enterprise software has grown, leading to significant declines in other software companies like Salesforce and Adobe. This trend is exacerbated by confirmations from firms like Accenture that AI is compressing demand for traditional IT services, making investors wary of software vendors, despite some counterarguments about their intrinsic value.

GOOGL.title: Berkshire Looked Like It Got a Bargain on Alphabet Stock. It Didn’t.
GOOGL.summary: Berkshire Hathaway's recent $10 billion purchase of Alphabet stock now appears less like a bargain, as Alphabet shares have fallen below the price Berkshire paid early this month. This transaction was part of Alphabet's larger $85 billion equity raise. The article highlights how market fluctuations can quickly change the perceived value of large investments.

GOOGL.title: Alphabet Stock Is Now Cheaper Than What Berkshire Paid
GOOGL.summary: Berkshire Hathaway's recent $10 billion investment in Alphabet stock has turned out to be less of a bargain than initially thought. The shares are now trading at a lower price than what Berkshire paid earlier this month. This situation arose from Alphabet's participation in an $85 billion equity raise.
